description: Result of governance validation produced by ConstitutionalValidator (src/body/services/constitutional_validator.py). GovernanceDecision is the state-conditional output of the risk/approval pipeline: risk_tier determines which approval_type is required. RiskTier is integer-valued (ROUTINE=1, STANDARD=3, ELEVATED=7, CRITICAL=10); ApprovalType is string-valued (autonomous/validation_only/human_confirmation/human_review) — divergent enum value types within the same governance domain, noted as reconciliation candidate. Governed vocabulary: risk_tier and approval_type enums in .intent/META/enums.json (ADR-056 D5). Authority: ADR-056 Wave 2 Session 1.

allowed.description: True if the governance check permits the operation to proceed.

risk_tier.description: Risk classification of the evaluated operation. Governed vocabulary: risk_tier enum in .intent/META/enums.json. Values: ROUTINE(1), STANDARD(3), ELEVATED(7), CRITICAL(10). Note: Python enum is integer-valued; string form uses uppercase name.

approval_type.description: Approval mechanism required for this risk tier. Governed vocabulary: approval_type enum in .intent/META/enums.json. Values: autonomous, validation_only, human_confirmation, human_review.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of the governance decision.

violations.description: List of constitutional violation messages that influenced this decision. Empty when allowed=True and no violations detected.

allOf.description: CRITICAL risk: approval_type must be human_review. Enforced by ConstitutionalValidator risk→approval mapping.
NOT ALLOWED: violations must be non-empty when allowed=False.
